text.title: Fine-tuning with LoRA (PEFT + TRL) Core Rules

rule.statement: LoRA target_modules MUST include q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj at minimum.
rule.rationale: Skipping projections collapses adapter capacity and quality.

rule.statement: LoRA rank MUST be in 8-64; rank 16 is the safe default for 7B/8B models.
rule.rationale: Rank <4 underfits; rank >128 burns memory with diminishing returns.

rule.statement: LoRA alpha MUST be 2× rank unless empirical evidence justifies otherwise.
rule.rationale: 2:1 alpha:rank is the empirically stable default.

rule.statement: Learning rate MUST be in 1e-4 to 3e-4 for AdamW; lower for SGD.
rule.rationale: Higher LRs diverge on LoRA; lower LRs underfit at 1-3 epochs.

rule.statement: Adapter weights MUST be saved separately by default; merge only when latency requires.
rule.rationale: Modular adapters let one base model serve many tenants.

rule.statement: Best-eval checkpoint MUST be promoted; not the final-epoch checkpoint.
rule.rationale: Loss continues falling after eval plateaus; final-epoch often overfits.
